---
title: "NetworkGeometryProcessor"
description: "Use NetworkGeometryProcessor as processor backend for alignment"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-07"
categories: [NetworkGeometryProcessor, Prediction, Graph]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# NetworkGeometryProcessor
This example shows how to plug [NetworkGeometryProcessor](../reference/NetworkGeometryProcessor.qmd) into [Aligner](../reference/Aligner.qmd).

## Why this processor

`NetworkGeometryProcessor` is generally slower than `DieussaertGeometryProcessor`, but it is more generic for network-like alignment scenarios and mixed geometry behavior.

## What the script does

1. Builds a processor with [ProcessorConfig](../reference/ProcessorConfig.qmd).
2. Injects that processor into the `Aligner`.
3. Loads one thematic geometry and GRB reference data.
4. Runs prediction over multiple distances.
5. Visualizes both the selected candidates and geometric change trend.

## Practical guidance

- Start with this processor only when your use case truly requires network behavior.
- Keep your distance series small first; expand it after validating quality.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Prediction with NetworkGeometryProcessor"
{{< include ../../examples/example_networkgeometryprocessor.py >}}
```
